channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Video shows Hurricane Milton-impacted family, dogs rescued from floodwaters
item.description: A family and their dogs were rescued from Hurricane Milton floodwaters in Florida, bodycamera footage from Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris zeros in on Black men, Trump focuses on women as both seek to fire up key voting blocs
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Kamala Harris and Donald Trump both pushed Tuesday to energize key constituencies that their allies worry might be slipping away, with the vice president looking to reach Black men and the former president focusing on women.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jets acquire Raiders wide receiver Davante Adams, AP sources say
item.description: The New York Jets have agreed to terms to acquire disgruntled wide receiver Davante Adams from the Las Vegas Raiders, reuniting him with quarterback Aaron Rodgers, two people with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Man allegedly killed by son,19, in California was renowned photojournalist
item.description: In a bizarre twist to a grisly crime on a popular hiking trail in the San Gabriel Mountains where a 19-year-old man allegedly stabbed his father to death, the victim has been identified as a well-known, award-winning war photographer, photojournalist and professor at the University of the Arts Londo
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Petitions for union representation doubled under Biden's presidency, first increase since 1970s
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There has been a doubling of petitions by workers to have union representation during President Joe Biden's administration, according to figures released Tuesday by the National Labor Relations Board.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Survivors and relatives in Maine's deadliest shooting start the process of suing the Army
item.description: LEWISTON, Maine (AP) — Lawyers for 100 survivors and relatives of victims of the deadliest shooting in Maine history have begun the formal process of suing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Geraldo Rivera backing Harris: Trump 'cannot be trusted to honor the Constitution'
item.description: Television personality Geraldo Rivera revealed Monday that he is backing Vice President Kamala Harris over former President Donald Trump in the election, now less than a month away.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: In Missouri, Halloween night signs were required in the yards of sex offenders. Until now
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — Many states have laws designed to keep children away from convicted sexual offenders on Halloween night, such as curfews for those on offender registries and requirements to keep their porch lights off. But a Missouri law mandating a yard sign was a step too far, a judge ruled.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Arkansas dad accused of killing man he found with daughter, 14, could use ‘heat of passion’ defense: attorney
item.description: Aaron Spencer, an Arkansas father accused of killing a man he found with his missing 14-year-old, will likely argue self-defense or that he was in the "heat of passion," expert says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Special report: Horse units patrol border from dusk till dawn
item.description: Border Report recently visited the Border Patrol's Rio Grande Valley Sector's horse patrol training facilities in Edinburg, Texas, and went on a night ride along with Border Patrol agents on horseback where several migrants trying to illegally cross the border were apprehended with the help of horse
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: North Carolina man accused of threatening FEMA workers with assault rifle amid Hurricane Helene clean-up
item.description: Authorities announced a North Carolina man was charged after being accused of threatening FEMA workers in western North Carolina working on recovery efforts from Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mississippi lineman helps with Hurricane Milton relief week before his wedding: 'I'm very proud of him'
item.description: A Mississippi lineman is putting others before his wedding day and helping with Hurricane Milton disaster recovery efforts in Florida the week of his wedding.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Ohio zoo CEO sentenced to prison in $2.3M fraud case
item.description: In July, Tom Stalf admitted to 12 counts of tampering with records and counts of theft by deception, conspiracy and telecommunication fraud.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: Coast Guard makes dramatic rescue at Oregon park
item.description: A dramatic rescue was captured on video as the US Coast Guard saved one person who became stuck on rocks in the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kustura, NewsNation

item.title: Camera catches cat's disappearance in a plastic bag
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) — An Ohio State University student is seeking justice and answers after her boyfriend’s roommate was caught on camera putting the couple’s cat into a plastic bag as it cried and then leaving their home.
item.dc:creator: Ava Boldizar, NewsNation

item.title: Man armed with rifle accused of threatening FEMA workers in North Carolina
item.description: A man accused of threatening federal disaster workers in western North Carolina has been charged, according to the Rutherford County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, WGN 9

item.title: Bath & Body Works apologizes for candle designed with what looked like KKK hoods
item.description: Bath & Body Works apologized for selling a winter-themed candle that came under scrutiny for label imagery that drew comparisons to Ku Klux Klan hoods, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: FEMA temporarily pauses aid in parts of NC over reported threats
item.description: The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) temporarily paused aid in parts of North Carolina this weekend after alleged threats targeted the agency’s personnel aiding in recovery efforts following Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Florence nonprofit group brings animals to safety after hurricanes
item.description: A group teamed up with Southwest Airlines for an emergency airlift of shelter animals out of Myrtle Beach.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Huffstickler, NewsNation

item.title: Major home improvement retailer files for bankruptcy, announces sale plans
item.description: True Value, a well-known home improvement company, entered into a sale agreement early Monday morning after filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: NJ Transit operator killed, passengers injured after train hits tree
item.description: An NJ Transit train operator is dead and nearly two dozen passengers are injured after a train struck a tree on the tracks Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KRON4

item.title: Group associated with polygamous sect builds fences on US Forest Service lands
item.description: A group in Colorado has fenced a parcel of U.S. Forest Service Land in an attempt to claim the land under the Homestead Act of 1862.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, Fox 5

item.title: Bath & Body Works apologizes for candle designed with what looked like KKK hoods
item.description: Bath & Body Works apologized for selling a winter-themed candle that came under scrutiny for label imagery that drew comparisons to Ku Klux Klan hoods, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WATCH: NASA launches Europa Clipper, will scour Jupiter moon for ingredients of life
item.description: Coverage of the launch is expected to begin at 11 a.m. ET and will be added to this story in the video player above. The launch window itself is set to open at 12:06 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press via Nexstar Media Wire, WGN 9

item.title: Armed man arrested near Trump rally denies 'assassination attempt' claims
item.description: “I’m an artist, I’m the last person that would cause any violence and harm to anybody,” the man has since said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Senate rankings: 5 seats most likely to flip
item.description: The battle for the Senate majority has hit its final month as Republicans race to end their four-year stint in the minority. The GOP needs to win only one of two competitive races in red states — Montana and Ohio — to do just that, barring a surprise loss elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Man with loaded gun arrested at checkpoint near Donald Trump's weekend rally in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Nevada man with a shotgun, loaded handgun, ammunition and several fake passports in his vehicle was arrested at a security checkpoint outside Donald Trump's rally Saturday night in the Southern California desert, authorities said. He was released the same day on $5,000 bail.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What $35 trillion in US national debt means for Americans
item.description: With the national debt standing at more than $35 trillion, economists fear that the country's debt load will only grow.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, ABC 4

item.title: What is the purpose of an open-front toilet seat?
item.description: Open-front toilet seats — or “U-shaped” seats — are common in public bathrooms or commercial spaces, but they’re almost never the norm in private residences.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: From the pulpit, Harris calls out Trump for hurricane misinformation. Biden surveys Florida's damage
item.description: GREENVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Kamala Harris used an appearance Sunday before a largely Black church audience in battleground North Carolina to call out Donald Trump for spreading misinformation about the government's hurricane response.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, AAMER MADHANI and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prison operator under federal scrutiny spent millions settling Tennessee mistreatment claims
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The leading private prison company in the U.S. has spent more than $4.4 million to settle dozens of complaints alleging mistreatment — including at least 22 inmate deaths — at its Tennessee prisons and jails since 2016.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, TRAVIS LOLLER and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Days left to claim cash in Oracle settlement that 'hundreds of millions' qualify for
item.description: The deadline is fast approaching for people to request a piece of the $115 million settlement fund Oracle agreed to pay out, settling claims it violated many people's privacy by selling their information to third parties.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Euthanasia, pronouns, sports betting: Intriguing 2024 ballot measures
item.description: Several states have placed important measures on their ballots, ranging from flag designs to end-of-life care.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, Fox 5

item.title: Will Donald Trump and Kamala Harris debate again?
item.description: While there was originally talk of more than one meeting between Trump and Harris, the former president made his intentions clear with a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chevy and GMC truck owners could get up to $12,700 as part of $35M settlement
item.description: Owners of the affected trucks could get anywhere from $400 to $12,712. Here's how to tell if you qualify.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KTLA 5

item.title: Floridians cleaning up from Hurricane Milton are hampered by a widespread fuel shortage
item.description: CORTEZ, Fla. (AP) — Floridians recovering from Hurricane Milton, many of whom were journeying home after fleeing hundreds of miles to escape the storm, spent much of Saturday searching for gas as a fuel shortage gripped the state. In St.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY ONEIL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Latest: Residents begin repairing damage from Hurricane Milton, which killed at least 10
item.description: Florida residents began repairing damage from Hurricane Milton, which smashed through coastal communities and tore homes to pieces, flooded streets and spawned a deadly tornadoes. At least 10 people are dead, but many expressed relief that Milton wasn’t worse.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
